title: Meta Ordered by E.U. to Alter ‘Addictive Design’ of Instagram and Facebook
summary: European Union authorities said the company’s use of “addictive design” violated a digital safety law.

title: Do Social Media Bans Work? + A Conversation About A.I. Consciousness + Tool Time
summary: “If the net result is that all the teens in Australia are still using social media, even after they’re technically banned from doing it, why are we doing any of this?”

title: A Top OpenAI Executive, Fidji Simo, Steps Down
summary: Ms. Simo, who was Sam Altman’s second in command before taking a medical leave in April, will become a part-time adviser to the company.

title: Meta Launches New A.I. Model as Global Technology Race Heats Up
summary: For the first time, the company will offer a paid version of the service, a departure from its longtime philosophy of giving its A.I. away for free.

title: Apple Sues OpenAI, Accusing It of Stealing Company Secrets
summary: The two companies struck a deal in 2024 to offer A.I. services on Apple devices, but their partnership has soured.

title: How Terrorist Groups Are Using A.I. to Gain an Edge in Battle
summary: A.I. chatbots are not just a propaganda tool for violent extremists but are aiding in bomb construction and attack planning, new research finds.

title: John Deere Farm Equipment Owners Have Right-to-Repair, F.T.C. Says
summary: A settlement by the company with the Federal Trade Commission will allow farmers and local mechanics to make their own fixes, instead of relying on authorized dealers.

title: Volkswagen Is Cutting Production as Sales in China Plunge
summary: The German automaker has struggled to compete with fast-growing Chinese companies that offer more affordable and sophisticated electric vehicles.

title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.6 Sol, Its Most Powerful AI Model Yet
summary: The new release was delayed after the U.S. government restricted the latest artificial intelligence models over cybersecurity concerns.

title: New York Times and Other Publishers Ask Court to Penalize OpenAI
summary: The Times, The New York Daily News and other media organizations accused OpenAI of withholding evidence in a lawsuit.

title: How to Turn Your Phone Into a Personal Health Dashboard
summary: Free apps from Google, Samsung and Apple can help you track your diet, exercise and well-being — and provide vital information during emergencies.

title: How to Prevent Meta From Using Your Instagram Images in A.I.
summary: The company’s new A.I. image generator has a surprising twist: It allows people to use images from public Instagram accounts.

title: In San Francisco, Some Home Sellers Now Ask for OpenAI or Anthropic Stock
summary: Even before OpenAI and Anthropic go public, they are distorting home sales in the San Francisco Bay Area, as people race to buy and sellers ask for stock instead of cash.

title: Meta Unveils an A.I. Image Generator
summary: Muse Image, which can create realistic images for users on Instagram and WhatsApp, is the company’s latest attempt to catch up in the global artificial intelligence race.

title: Businesses Fear Politicization as Trump Gains More Power Over U.S. Agencies
summary: A Supreme Court ruling that presidents can fire independent regulators without cause has added volatility for industries that prefer stable enforcement.

title: Why A.I. Distillation Has Become a Hot Topic in the Race with China
summary: U.S. companies complain that competitors in China are unfairly copying their A.I. systems using a technique that has been around for years.

title: Microsoft Lays Off Thousands of Xbox Employees, Closes Game Studios
summary: The layoffs were part of wider cuts at Microsoft, as the company prioritizes spending on artificial intelligence.

title: How Meta’s Threads Became as Popular as X
summary: The social platform that Meta once positioned as a rival to Elon Musk’s X now has 500 million monthly users. It increasingly resembles Reddit.

title: Fable Ban Reversed + Dr. Dana Suskind on Parenting With A.I. + Prediction Market Drama
summary: What we learned from the government’s biggest attempt yet to control who can gain access to the most powerful new A.I. models.

title: Microsoft Disclosure Provides Rare Glimpse of Tax Haven Tactics
summary: Other U.S. companies will soon need to provide similar reports as a new European directive takes effect.
